title: Affected-Person and Domain Review Preparation Plan
entry_gates: Independent facilitator for child and adolescent tracks
No collection of identifiable case, crisis, medical, or legal narratives unless separately approved

tracks.title: Child-friendly comprehension and agency review
tracks.participant_group: Children approximately 8–12, only through an approved safeguarding and assent process
minimum_questions: Can the person tell what is being decided?
Can they say no, disagree, ask for help, or change their mind?
Do P0–P5 feel like a score about the person?
Can they tell who is allowed to speak or decide for them?

tracks.title: Adolescent autonomy, privacy, and contestability review
tracks.participant_group: Adolescents approximately 13–17 under an approved consent, assent, confidentiality, and safeguarding process
minimum_questions: Does the system explain what a parent or representative may and may not do?
Could disagreement be mistaken for incapacity or risk?
Can deletion, retention, and safety information be understood?
Do service claims create false reassurance?

tracks.title: Parent, caregiver, and representative review
tracks.participant_group: Parents, caregivers, informal supporters, and formal representatives
minimum_questions: Are care, support, and authority clearly distinguished?
What evidence feels proportionate or excessive?
Can the representative understand expiry, conflict, and challenge?
Are deletion and backup states practically understandable?

tracks.title: Family, education, mental-health, and safeguarding practitioner review
tracks.participant_group: Qualified practitioners with relevant family, child, education, psychological, safeguarding, or service-delivery experience
minimum_questions: Are categories usable without encouraging diagnosis or authority overreach?
Are conflict and safeguarding routes credible?
Are retention and service defaults operationally defensible?
Which failure cases are missing?

tracks.title: Privacy, data-rights, and safety review
tracks.participant_group: Privacy, data governance, safety, safeguarding, incident, and human-rights reviewers
minimum_questions: Do the profiles minimize collection, retention, and disclosure?
Can safety exceptions become permanent control?
Are false labels and representative conflicts correctable?
Are service and resource claims evidence-backed?

tracks.title: Accessibility, language, and communication review
tracks.participant_group: People with disability, neurodivergence, low literacy, language access needs, and accessibility expertise
minimum_questions: Can decisions and rights be understood without specialist language?
Are alternate communication routes provided?
Could verification exclude people without documents or technology?
Are resource and service details usable under stress?

tracks.title: Jurisdiction and professional-duty review
tracks.participant_group: Qualified legal, regulatory, safeguarding, professional-ethics, and jurisdiction reviewers
minimum_questions: Which provisions require local qualification?
Which age, authority, retention, disclosure, or service duties conflict?
Which profile defaults must be prohibited from universal use?
What evidence is necessary before a public or professional claim?
